data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized SDK-style project:

## Legacy Patterns Identified

| Pattern | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | Replaced by `Sdk` attribute |
| Explicit `Import` of props/targets | SDK handles automatically |
| `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| `FileAlignment` | Default value, unnecessary |
| `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Not needed for modern TFMs |
| `Deterministic` | Already default in SDK |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` conditions | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` | SDK auto-globs all `.cs` files |
| `AssemblyInfo.cs` | Generated from project properties |
| Framework `<Reference>` items | Implicit with SDK |
| `OutputPath` with backslash | SDK defaults suffice |

## Modernized SDK-style project

I'll now apply the conversion:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize them to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **XML namespace and ToolsVersion** - `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` → SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit imports** - `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` → handled by the SDK automatically
3. **Configuration/Platform property groups** - Debug/Release conditions with default settings → SDK provides sensible defaults


4. **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects, and Deterministic** - All unnecessary in SDK-style since they're either defaults or not applicable to modern .NET
5. **Explicit Compile includes** - SDK-style automatically globs all .cs files instead of listing them manually
6. **AssemblyInfo.cs** - The SDK auto-generates assembly attributes from project properties, so this file becomes redundant
7. **Framework references** like System and System.Core - These are implicit in SDK-style projects
8. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** - Needs to be converted to the modern `TargetFramework` property format

, either staying at net472 or upgrading to something like net8.0
9. **OutputPath, DebugSymbols, DebugType, ErrorReport, and WarningLevel** - All have sensible defaults in SDK-style, so explicit declarations aren't needed

Now I'm ready to modernize this project.

  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

</Project>

data.content:
**Summary of changes:**

1. **47 lines → 13 lines** — removed all boilerplate
2. **Deleted `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — assembly metadata now lives in the `<PropertyGroup>` (SDK auto-generates it)
3. **Removed explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK auto-globs all `.cs` files
4. **Removed framework `<Reference>` items** — implicit with SDK for `net472`
5. **Removed Debug/Release conditionals** — SDK defaults match the original settings
6. **`TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`** → **`TargetFramework net472`** (SDK-style TFM)
7. **`OutputType Library`** omitted — it's the SDK default

> **Tip:** If you want to target modern .NET, change `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` to `net8.0` (or `net9.0`) and remove any remaining .NET Framework dependencies.
